title: Primary happy path
p3_observations: P3: happy_path_receipt_fields is a required-subset (10 of 13 receipt keys); all 13 keys are nonetheless fully validated by the result assertion — consistent with the S06 audit_hint_fields pattern; no fix needed.
P3: contract future_subphases.review_required_routing (S14) is unenforced roadmap metadata carried forward from S06; no fix needed.

Informational: the result/receipt does not strictly reject EXTRA receipt keys, but the producer freezes the object with exactly the expected keys and the fixture matches; consistent with prior subphases.
Informational: validator self-runs the full S01->S07 precheck chain plus firm-level and negative cases, providing strong executable coverage beyond static field checks.
review_text:
I've completed a thorough static review of the entire diff plus the surrounding repository context. Let me confirm my findings before producing the verdict.

Key verifications performed:
- **S06 dependency** (`service.js:2289+`): `precheckControlPlaneClaudeReviewPrimaryHappyPath` requires `audit_hint_precheck_subphase_id === "RP00.P02.M04.S06"`, a present `audit_hint` object, a nonblank `audit_event_ref`, carried `audit_hint_decision === "allow_metadata_only"` + `audit_event_ref_satisfied === true`, and re-runs `assertControlPlaneClaudeReviewAuditHintPrecheckResult` for defense-in-depth. The S06 producer (`service.js:2218-2287`) emits exactly those markers, so the chain binds correctly.
- **Metadata-only / no side effects**: the new function is pure — validate → reject claims → build frozen receipt+result → assert → return. No runtime Claude call, no queue/audit/AuthZ/permission-engine/service/route/product writes; all 12 capability flags forced `false` and `no_real_data: true`, enforced in policy, prechecker, result validator, fixture, and contract.
- **Fail-closed claims**: any non-`false` value (including falsy `0`) on a forbidden claim throws; any unknown claim throws. Wrong entrypoint/model/effort/read-only/marker/missing hint/missing ref/denied/unsatisfied all throw.
- **Alignment**: contract definition, policy module, prechecker, validators, README, fixture, and `validate-rp00-control-plane-contract.mjs` all updated consistently; the validator runs an end-to-end S01→S07 chain plus negative cases and reuses `expected*`/`claudeReviewAuditHintPrecheckPolicyModule` variables defined in the S06 block (no ReferenceError).
- **S08 handoff**: `next_subphase`/`secondaryWorkflowPath` = `RP00.P02.M04.S08` everywhere; `completesSourceMicroPhase: false`; `does_not_complete_RP00_P02_M04`/`RP00_P02`/`close_RP00`. `future_subphases.review_required_routing: "RP00.P02.M04.S14"` matches the established S06 pattern.

No P0/P1/P2 issues found.

```json
{
  "verdict": "GO",
  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "P3",
      "file": "packages/control-plane/src/service.js",
      "line": 1087,
      "issue": "happy_path_receipt is built with 13 keys (tenant_id, actor_id, action_id, object_id, matter_id, permission_decision_ref, audit_event_ref, audit_hint_precheck_ref, audit_hint_policy_ref, decision_reason, working_product_behavior_ref, effect, next_subphase) but happy_path_receipt_fields / policy.requiredHappyPathReceiptFields enumerate only 10 (matter_id, audit_hint_policy_ref, decision_reason are omitted). The '_fields' list is a required-subset, not an exhaustive key listing.",
      "recommendation": "No action required. This mirrors the established S06 audit_hint / audit_hint_fields convention, and assertControlPlaneClaudeReviewPrimaryHappyPathResult independently asserts all 13 receipt keys (validators.js ~1411-1429), so there is no validation gap. Noted only for transparency."
    },
    {
      "severity": "P3",
      "file": "contracts/control-plane-contract.json",
      "line": 370,
      "issue": "control_plane_claude_review_primary_happy_path_definition.future_subphases carries review_required_routing: 'RP00.P02.M04.S14', which is not present on the runtime policy module (CONTROL_PLANE_CLAUDE_REVIEW_PRIMARY_HAPPY_PATH_POLICY.futureSubphases only carries secondaryWorkflowPath) and is not enforced by the validator.",
      "recommendation": "No action required. This roadmap pointer is carried forward verbatim from the S06 definition (which used the same review_required_routing: 'RP00.P02.M04.S14'), so it is consistent with the established convention. The validator intentionally enforces only secondary_workflow_path = S08."
    }
  ],
  "required_fixes": [],
  "deferred_or_informational": [
    "P3: happy_path_receipt_fields is a required-subset (10 of 13 receipt keys); all 13 keys are nonetheless fully validated by the result assertion — consistent with the S06 audit_hint_fields pattern; no fix needed.",
    "P3: contract future_subphases.review_required_routing (S14) is unenforced roadmap metadata carried forward from S06; no fix needed.",
    "Informational: the result/receipt does not strictly reject EXTRA receipt keys, but the producer freezes the object with exactly the expected keys and the fixture matches; consistent with prior subphases.",
    "Informational: validator self-runs the full S01->S07 precheck chain plus firm-level and negative cases, providing strong executable coverage beyond static field checks."
  ],
  "review_summary": "RP00.P02.M04.S07 implements a metadata-only Claude review Primary happy path that strictly consumes the S06 audit hint precheck result and emits a synthetic success receipt. precheckControlPlaneClaudeReviewPrimaryHappyPath is a pure function: it validates the S06 input (entrypoint=claude_review, audit_hint_precheck_subphase_id=RP00.P02.M04.S06, present audit_hint, nonblank audit_event_ref, carried allow_metadata_only decision + satisfied audit_event_ref, and a full re-assertion of the S06 result), rejects every forbidden and unknown happy-path claim (fail-closed on any non-false value, including falsy 0), then returns a frozen receipt+result with all runtime/write capabilities forced false and no_real_data=true. The S06 dependency is correctly bound and re-verified for defense-in-depth. No runtime Claude invocation, no review queue/assignment/notification writes, no audit ledger/event writes, no AuthZ evaluator or permission-engine/runtime-permission execution, no service logic, no runtime route, and no product-state writes are permitted at any layer (policy, prechecker, result validator, fixture, contract). Policy, prechecker, validators (assertControlPlaneClaudeReviewPrimaryHappyPathPolicy/Result), README, synthetic fixture, contract definition (with subphase_closeouts entry, current_subphase pointer, acceptance-gate flag control_plane_claude_review_primary_happy_path_verified, and construction-inspection gate), and the contract validator are all aligned; the validator additionally executes the full S01->S07 chain plus firm-level and negative cases, and reuses variables already defined in the closed S06 block (no ReferenceError). S08 handoff is recorded consistently (next_subphase/secondaryWorkflowPath=RP00.P02.M04.S08), with completesSourceMicroPhase=false and RP00.P02.M04 / RP00.P02 / RP00 explicitly left open. Only two P3 informational observations (a required-subset receipt-fields list and an unenforced roadmap pointer), both consistent with the established S06 convention and requiring no changes. Verdict: GO."
}
```

findings.title: happy_path_receipt is built with 13 keys (tenant_id, actor_id, action_id, object_id, matter_id, permission_decision_ref, audit_event_ref, audit_hint_precheck_ref, audit_hint_policy_ref, decision_reason, working_product_behavior_ref, effect, next_subphase) but happy_path_receipt_fields / policy.requiredHappyPathReceiptFields enumerate only 10 (matter_id, audit_hint_policy_ref, decision_reason are omitted). The '_fields' list is a required-subset, not an exhaustive key listing.
findings.body: No action required. This mirrors the established S06 audit_hint / audit_hint_fields convention, and assertControlPlaneClaudeReviewPrimaryHappyPathResult independently asserts all 13 receipt keys (validators.js ~1411-1429), so there is no validation gap. Noted only for transparency.

findings.title: control_plane_claude_review_primary_happy_path_definition.future_subphases carries review_required_routing: 'RP00.P02.M04.S14', which is not present on the runtime policy module (CONTROL_PLANE_CLAUDE_REVIEW_PRIMARY_HAPPY_PATH_POLICY.futureSubphases only carries secondaryWorkflowPath) and is not enforced by the validator.
findings.body: No action required. This roadmap pointer is carried forward verbatim from the S06 definition (which used the same review_required_routing: 'RP00.P02.M04.S14'), so it is consistent with the established convention. The validator intentionally enforces only secondary_workflow_path = S08.
